method: The CFD-VisQA benchmark is organized along three orthogonal axes. Axis 1 (Flow Scenarios): 10 canonical thermal-fluid scenarios spanning natural convection (heated vertical plate, differentially heated cavity, Rayleigh-Benard), forced convection (laminar channel, turbulent channel, backward-facing step, lid-driven cavity), mixed convection (mixed convection channel, heated obstacle), and indoor airflow (ventilated room). Axis 2 (Visualization Types): 6 standardized rendering types — velocity contour, temperature contour, pressure contour, vector field, streamlines, and composite overlay. Axis 3 (Question Difficulty): 5 levels from L1 visual reading through L5 cross-field reasoning, with L3 physical plausibility judgment as the primary evaluation task. Each scenario includes 1 correct case and 5 error variants spanning 6 error types (E1 under-convergence, E2 BC swap, E3 wrong viscosity, E4 wrong turbulence model, E5 coarse mesh, E8 gravity flip), yielding 60 total CFD cases, 258 flow field images, and 279 physics-grounded questions.
caption: Three-axis structure of the CFD-VisQA benchmark: 10 flow scenarios, 6 visualization types, and 5 question difficulty levels, with 6 error types applied per scenario. The benchmark comprises 60 OpenFOAM cases, 258 images, and 279 questions.
